description: Add per-user image search source toggles to Settings (React web + Swift iOS). Users can enable or disable Walmart, Spoonacular, Open Food Facts, and SerpAPI individually. Preferences are stored as a JSONB column on the profiles table. The search-products edge function respects a sources param and returns grouped results. The ImagePicker renders per-service sections and shows a Settings link when all sources are off.

userStories.title: Add image_search_settings column to profiles
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a migration that adds image_search_settings to the profiles table so user preferences can be persisted and synced across devices.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration file created at supabase/migrations/{timestamp}_add_image_search_settings_to_profiles.sql
Column definition: image_search_settings jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{"walmart":true,"spoonacular":false,"openfoodfacts":false,"serpapi":false}'::jsonb
ALTER TABLE profiles ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS pattern used for safety
Existing rows receive the default value via the column default (no explicit backfill needed)
RLS policies on profiles are unchanged — users can already read and update their own row
Migration runs cleanly against the local Supabase instance (supabase db reset succeeds)
npm run lint passes with no new errors
npm run build succeeds

userStories.title: React: load and save image_search_settings in profile service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the React app to read image_search_settings from the user's profile and expose a function to update it, so Settings and ImagePicker components can consume and persist preferences.
acceptanceCriteria: AuthContext.jsx: the profiles SELECT query adds image_search_settings to the selected columns — select('id, display_name, email, avatar_url, created_at, timezone, image_search_settings')

The profile object in context includes imageSearchSettings (camelCased, mapping from the snake_case column name image_search_settings returned by Supabase — this mapping must be applied explicitly when constructing the profile object from the query response)
A new exported function updateImageSearchSettings(userId, settings) is added to src/services/database.js (or the appropriate service file following existing patterns)
settings is an object with keys walmart, spoonacular, openfoodfacts, serpapi (all booleans)
Performs a Supabase update on profiles where id = userId
Returns { error } — callers handle errors
If image_search_settings is null on the profile (pre-migration rows), the app falls back to { walmart: true, spoonacular: false, openfoodfacts: false, serpapi: false } rather than crashing
npm run lint passes with no new errors
npm run build succeeds

userStories.title: React: Image Search Settings UI in MobileSettings
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see an Image Search section in Settings where I can toggle each search service on or off, so I have control over which sources are used when I pick an item image.
acceptanceCriteria: A new Image Search section is added to MobileSettings.jsx below the Appearance section
The section contains four toggle rows using the existing .toggleRow / .toggle / .toggleTrack CSS pattern: Walmart, Spoonacular, Open Food Facts, SerpAPI
Each toggle's checked state is driven by profile.imageSearchSettings[key] from AuthContext
Toggling a switch immediately calls updateImageSearchSettings(userId, updatedSettings) and optimistically updates local state
On save error, the toggle reverts to its previous state and a brief error message is shown (matching existing error handling patterns in the component)
The section header reads Image Search (matching existing .sectionHeader style)
No toggle prevents disabling the last active source — all four can be turned off simultaneously
npm run lint passes with no new errors
npm run build succeeds

userStories.title: Edge function: respect sources param and return grouped results
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the search-products edge function to accept a sources query param, skip disabled services, and return results grouped by source so the client can render per-service sections.
acceptanceCriteria: The edge function at supabase/functions/search-products/index.ts accepts an optional sources query param — a comma-separated list of enabled service keys: e.g., sources=walmart,serpapi
When sources is provided, only the listed services are called. Unlisted services are skipped entirely (not called, not waited on)
When sources is absent or empty, all services run as today (backward-compatible default — all four enabled)
The parallel Promise.all for the three primary sources (Walmart, Spoonacular, Open Food Facts) is filtered to only include enabled sources before the call
SerpAPI: only called sequentially when it is in the enabled list AND all enabled primary sources returned empty
If sources contains only serpapi, SerpAPI is called directly without running any primary source
Response shape changes from flat { results, source } to grouped: { groups: [{ source, label, results }] }
Only sources that returned at least one result are included in groups (empty-result sources are omitted)
Group order matches source priority order: Walmart → Spoonacular → Open Food Facts → SerpAPI
Each ProductResult retains its existing shape: { url, thumbnail, title }
Clients that pass no sources param receive all non-empty groups (backward-compatible in behavior, new in shape — all callers updated in S-5 and S-7)
This story must not be deployed to production until S-5 (React client) and S-7 (iOS client) are also ready to deploy. All three should be released atomically, or S-5 and S-7 must ship first
deno lint supabase/functions/search-products/index.ts passes

userStories.title: React: pass sources, render per-service sections, empty-state when all off
userStories.description: As a user, I want image search results grouped by service so I can see which source each image comes from. When all sources are off, I want a clear message in the search tab with a link to Settings to enable them.
acceptanceCriteria: src/services/imageSearch.js: searchImages(query, count, enabledSources) gains a third parameter defaulting to all-enabled for backward compatibility
Builds a comma-separated sources param from keys where value is true and appends to the edge function URL
If all values are false, returns [] immediately without calling the edge function
Returns response.groups (always an array) from the edge function response
Cache key is derived from both query and the sorted sources string (e.g. query:sourcesString) so changing enabled sources invalidates prior cached results for the same query
ImagePicker: the Search online tab is always rendered (never hidden)

When allSourcesOff is true and user is on search tab: show empty-state message and a button/link that navigates to Settings using the existing in-app routing pattern. No search input or results grid rendered in this state
When sources are active: results render as per-service sections with a label heading per group. Images within each section render in the existing grid/thumbnail style
Sections with zero results are not rendered
If edge function returns [] (all enabled sources returned nothing), display the existing No images found empty state
ImagePicker receives imageSearchSettings prop (object with four boolean keys) and onNavigateToSettings callback prop
ImagePicker.propTypes updated: imageSearchSettings: PropTypes.object, onNavigateToSettings: PropTypes.func
All callers of ImagePicker pass imageSearchSettings={profile?.imageSearchSettings} and an appropriate onNavigateToSettings handler
npm run lint passes with no new errors
npm run build succeeds

userStories.title: Swift iOS: profile model + settings persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the iOS app to read and write image_search_settings from the user's Supabase profile so the Swift app respects the same preferences as the web app.
acceptanceCriteria: The Swift Profile (or equivalent) model gains an imageSearchSettings: ImageSearchSettings property, where ImageSearchSettings is a Codable struct with four Bool fields: walmart, spoonacular, openfoodfacts, serpapi
Default value when the column is null or the key is missing: ImageSearchSettings(walmart: true, spoonacular: false, openfoodfacts: false, serpapi: false)
The Supabase profile fetch query includes image_search_settings in the selected columns
A updateImageSearchSettings(_ settings: ImageSearchSettings) async throws function is added to the profile/auth service layer, updating the profiles row via Supabase Swift client

No CodingKeys are needed — the JSONB keys (walmart, spoonacular, openfoodfacts, serpapi) are already lowercase and match the Swift property names directly. Swift's default decoding handles them without explicit key mapping
Project builds successfully

userStories.title: Swift iOS: settings UI toggles + ProductSearchService + image picker sections
userStories.description: As a user on iOS, I want the same image search toggles available in the native settings screen, results grouped by service in the image picker, and a Settings link when all sources are off.
acceptanceCriteria: An Image Search section is added to the iOS settings screen with four Toggle rows: Walmart, Spoonacular, Open Food Facts, SerpAPI
Each toggle is bound to the corresponding field in the user's imageSearchSettings
Toggling calls updateImageSearchSettings and optimistically updates local state; on error, reverts and shows an alert matching existing iOS error handling patterns
ProductSearchService.searchProducts(query:count:) gains a sources: ImageSearchSettings parameter
Builds the sources query param string from enabled keys and appends to the edge function URL
If all sources are disabled, returns [] immediately without a network call
Parses the new groups response shape into [ProductResultGroup] where each group has source: String, label: String, and results: [ProductResult]
The iOS image picker renders results as a list of sections, one per group, with the group label as the section header. Images within each section render in the existing grid style
When all sources are off, the search UI shows an empty-state message with a button that navigates to the Settings screen using the existing in-app navigation pattern
Project builds successfully
